column1: Have you heard of the English city of York? It's one of the most____________cities in the UK. (FAME)
The Romans started the city in 71AD. It quickly grew to become____________to the Roman Empire as a military base. (CENTRE)
In 211, Constantine I was crowned Emperor in York. This was__________unusual and special, as Emperors were almost always crowned in Rome. (REAL)
The historical______________of York is also symbolised by the Minster, one of the largest churches in the world. (IMPORTANT)
The city burnt down in a________________fire in 1137, bur managed to recover to become a centre of trade and commerce bu the 1500s. (DISASTER)
Fire struck again in 1984 and the Minster was badly damaged, but_____________, like the city itself, the Minster recovered its former glory to stand tall once again. (FORTUNE)
I grew up in the UK in the 1980s. During my____________there wasn't always a lot to do in the summer holidays. (CHILD)
On the rare occasions when it was a warm___________day, we'd got for a picnic on Blackpool beach. (SUN)
As far as my family were concerned, there was nothing more_____________than eating sandwiches on the beach. (ENJOY)
We hardly ever went swimming, though, as the water was often____________cold. So we would just play on the wet sand. (EXTREME)
But even if we were___________with the weather and it started raining, we could always go for some fish and chips. (LUCKY)
Nothing gave us more pleasure after a____________day at the seaside. (TIRE)
